title: What to watch for the US economy in H2 2026
gist: Oxford Economics expects US inflation to decelerate and GDP growth to reaccelerate in H2 2026 on falling oil prices, with geopolitical tensions and the durability of AI-driven optimism as the key risks to the call.

insights.claim: US headline PCE inflation is projected to fall from a May peak of 4.2% to 3.7% in June, with further deceleration expected in H2 2026 as energy prices decline.
insights.speaker: Keith Mason
insights.so_what: A confirmed inflation rollover from a 4.2% peak supports a friendlier rate path; watch monthly PCE prints to validate the disinflation before positioning for cuts.
insights.quote: headline PCE inflation projected to fall from a peak of 4.2% in May to 3.7% in June
insights.locator: In the first half of the year

insights.claim: Falling oil prices are the primary driver expected to ease US inflationary pressures in the second half of 2026.
insights.speaker: Keith Mason
insights.so_what: The disinflation thesis is levered to energy; a reversal in oil would undercut the entire soft-landing narrative, so treat crude as the key macro tell.
insights.locator: The economic landscape for the second half

insights.claim: US economic growth slowed to nearly 2% annually in H1 2026 while the labour market stabilised despite the Iran war, and growth is expected to reaccelerate in H2.
insights.speaker: Keith Mason
insights.so_what: A stable labour market plus expected reacceleration argues against recession positioning into H2; watch for the growth pickup to materialise before adding cyclical risk.
insights.quote: economic growth slowed to nearly 2% annually, stabilising the labour market despite challenges posed by the Iran war
insights.locator: In the first half of the year

insights.claim: Continued growth in AI investment is expected to have significant implications for US productivity and consumer spending.
insights.speaker: Keith Mason
insights.so_what: AI capex is being framed as a macro productivity and demand driver, not just an equity story; its persistence is flagged as a swing factor for the whole outlook.
insights.locator: Investment in AI continues to grow

insights.claim: The Federal Reserve remains cautious on rate hikes, but any resurgence in energy prices or inflation could prompt a reassessment of monetary policy.
insights.speaker: Keith Mason
insights.so_what: The Fed is data-dependent and asymmetrically sensitive to an energy-driven inflation rebound; a crude spike is the scenario that flips the policy stance hawkish.
insights.locator: While the Federal Reserve remains cautious

insights.claim: The two critical risks to the US H2 2026 outlook are geopolitical tensions and the stability of AI-driven optimism.
insights.speaker: Keith Mason
insights.so_what: Both key risks are sentiment/tail-driven rather than in the baseline; a break in AI optimism or a new geopolitical shock is what invalidates the benign scenario.
